title: picris angustifolia subsp. carolorum-henricorum
intro: Picris angustifolia subsp. carolorum-henricorum, also known as a plant species within the Picris genus, is a type of bitter thistle or hawk's beard, characterized by its narrow leaves and prickly stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Picris angustifolia subsp. carolorum-henricorum include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, with some practitioners also using it in ointments and salves for topical applications.
